revision.text:
{{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient burial mounds, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is approximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the burial mound. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Address''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient burial mounds, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is approximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the burial mound. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Address''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient burial mounds, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is approximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the burial mound. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Address''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient burial mounds, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is approximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the burial mound. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Address''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient burial mounds, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is approximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the burial mound. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Address''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * revision.comment: Text replacement - "長持形石棺}}" to "長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}"
revision.text:
{{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient burial mounds, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is approximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the burial mound. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Address''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kyoto Prefecture history]] {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient burial mounds, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is approximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the burial mound. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Address''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient burial mounds, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is approximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the burial mound. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Address''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient burial mounds, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is approximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the burial mound. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Address''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == revision.text:
{{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient burial mounds, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is approximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the burial mound. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Address''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kutsukawa {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient burial mounds, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is approximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the burial mound. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Address''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient burial mounds, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is approximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the burial mound. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Address''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient burial mounds, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is approximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the burial mound. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Address''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient burial mounds, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early miqqle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is approximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the burial mound. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Aqqress''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient burial mounds, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early miqqle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is aqqroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the burial mound. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Aqqress''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early miqqle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is aqqroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the burial mound. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Aqqress''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early miqqle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is aqqroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Aqqress''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early miqqle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is aqqroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Aqqress''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early miqqle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is aqqroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Aqqress''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) |Group = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |Latitude_deg = 34 |Latitude_min = 51 |Latitude_sec = 53.32 |Longitude_deg = 135 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 44.19 |ISO = JP-26 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length 180 m |Construction_period = Early 5th century |Burial_facility = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Buried_individual = |Artifacts = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |Notes = |Map = Japan Kyoto |Label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |Label_position = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been designated as a {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. Artifacts excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early miqqle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is aqqroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic Site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == Artifacts == * '''Sarcophagus Artifacts''' - Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) - Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' - Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic Site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group designation) == location == '''Aqqress''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links ==
