revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus other_designation1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been other_designation1 as a {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National other_designation. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == other_designation == === [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === other_designation === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group other_designation) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus other_designation1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been other_designation1 as a {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National other_designation. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == other_designation == === [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === other_designation === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group other_designation) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus other_designation1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been other_designation1 as a {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National other_designation. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important Cultural Property. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == other_designation == === [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === other_designation === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group other_designation) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National other_designation Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Plattype-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus other_designation1 as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been other_designation1 as a {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National other_designation. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == other_designation == === [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === other_designation === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group other_designation) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} of Japan]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture history]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=draft}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線|12=draft}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|12=draft}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|12=draft}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅|12=draft}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|12=draft}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)|12=draft}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture history]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}}

Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=draft}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線|12=draft}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|12=draft}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|12=draft}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅|12=draft}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|12=draft}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)|12=draft}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture history]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=draft}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線|12=draft}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|12=draft}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|12=draft}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅|12=draft}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|12=draft}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)|12=draft}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture history]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=draft}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線|12=draft}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|12=draft}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|12=draft}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅|12=draft}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|12=draft}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)|12=draft}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture history]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|12=draft}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅|12=draft}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|12=draft}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)|12=draft}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture history]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Important designation1 (Japan)]] {{Kofun navbar}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=draft}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線|12=draft}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|12=draft}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|12=draft}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅|12=draft}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|12=draft}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)|12=draft}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture history]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Important designation1 (Japan)]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|12=draft}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=draft}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|12=draft}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Group" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Group|ja|久津川古墳群|12=draft}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線|12=draft}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|12=draft}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=draft}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Group and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Group Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|12=draft}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅|12=draft}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|12=draft}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)|12=draft}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} of Japan]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture history]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]]

{{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|12=draft}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅|12=draft}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|12=draft}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)|12=draft}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Group}} of Japan]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture history]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]]
