revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōyō City|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1 (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōyō City|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1 (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Line (JR West)|qq=}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|qq=}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Kyoto Line|qq=}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Station|qq=}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Nara Line|qq=}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinden Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture history]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōyō City|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1 (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōyō City|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1 (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Line (JR West)|qq=}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|qq=}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Bashōzuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Kyoto Line|qq=}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Station|qq=}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Nara Line|qq=}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinden Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture history]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōyō City|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1 (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Line (JR West)|qq=}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|qq=}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Kyoto Line|qq=}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Station|qq=}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Nara Line|qq=}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinden Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture history]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]]

The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Kyoto Line|qq=}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Station|qq=}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōyō City|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1 (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō City|ja|城陽市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōyō City|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important designation1 (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1 (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Line (JR West)|qq=}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|qq=}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kintetsu Kyoto Line|ja|近鉄京都線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Kyoto Line|qq=}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|ja|久津川駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Station|qq=}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Nara Line|qq=}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|ja|新田駅 (京都府)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinden Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|qq=}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|qq=}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:久津川古墳群]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]]

It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|qq=}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:久津川古墳群]] [[Category:京都府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]]

It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|qq=}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:久津川古墳群]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]]

This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|qq=}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:久津川古墳群]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|qq=}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:久津川古墳群]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|qq=}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:久津川古墳群]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 51 |coordinates = 53.32 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 46 |coordinates = 44.19 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|qq=}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kuzugawa Kofun Group]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}}
