Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = {{Coord|34.864833|N|135.778972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}} }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|qq=}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}}

Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}} }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|qq=|1=Kizu River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28196671}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}}

Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|qq=|1=Kizu River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28196671}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] }} [[sn:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:utsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] [[en:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11369464}}

Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = {{Coord|34.864833|N|135.778972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|qq=|1=Kizu River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28196671}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11369464}}

Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = {{Coord|34.864833|N|135.778972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|qq=|1=Kizu River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28196671}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11369464}}

Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = {{Coord|34.864833|N|135.778972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|1=Kizu River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28196671}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11369464}}

Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = {{Coord|34.864833|N|135.778972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|1=Kizu River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28196671}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11369464}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = {{Coord|34.864833|N|135.778972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|1=Kizu River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28196671}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11369464}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = {{Coord|34.864833|N|135.778972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|1=Kizu River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28196671}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11369464}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = {{Coord|34.864833|N|135.778972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|1=Kizu River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28196671}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11369464}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = {{Coord|34.864833|N|135.778972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|1=Kizu River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28196671}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11369464}} [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = {{Coord|34.864833|N|135.778972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, [[:en:Jōyō, Kyoto|Jōyō City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The mound is now intersected by the [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|Nara Line (JR West)]] railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|1=Kizu River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28196671}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - [[:en:Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]] [[:en:Kutsukawa Station|Kutsukawa Station]] (10 minutes on foot) - [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] [[:en:Shinden Station (Kyoto)|Shinden Station (Kyoto)]] (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11369464}} [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun]][[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] {{Ancient {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō, Kyoto|en|Jōyō, Kyoto|WD=Q817276|lt=Jōyō City}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = {{Coord|34.864833|N|135.778972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō, Kyoto|en|Jōyō, Kyoto|WD=Q817276|lt=Jōyō City}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|en|Nara Line (JR West)|WD=Q1157044|lt=Nara Line (JR West)}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|1=Kizu River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28196671}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|en|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|en|Kutsukawa Station|WD=Q6448547|lt=Kutsukawa Station}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|en|Nara Line (JR West)|WD=Q1157044|lt=JR Nara Line}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|en|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|WD=Q974100|lt=Shinden Station (Kyoto)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11369464}} [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

Mound (back circular part on the left, front square part on the right) | part_of = {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Jōyō, Kyoto|en|Jōyō, Kyoto|WD=Q817276|lt=Jōyō City|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, Hirakawa Kurumazuka |coordinates = {{Coord|34.864833|N|135.778972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |area = Mound length 180 m |built = Early 5th century |burial = Back circular part: Direct interment with a {{ill|Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Long chest-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}; West {{ill|Tsunedashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunedashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}: Direct interment with a composite {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Numerous burial goods, fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |Historic_site = National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}, "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" |Cultural_property = Mirrors and sarcophagus Historic site as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Kutsukawa Kurumazuka
Kofun |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun-SSW |map_dot_label = Top |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun''' (久津川車塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in Hirakawa Kurumazuka, {{ill|Jōyō, Kyoto|en|Jōyō, Kyoto|WD=Q817276|lt=Jōyō City|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, a cluster of ancient Kofuns, and has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}. burial goods excavated from the site, including mirrors and a sarcophagus, are classified as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. This is the largest kofun in the southern Yamashiro area and is estimated to have been constructed in the early 5th century during the early middle{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The mound is now intersected by the {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|en|Nara Line (JR West)|WD=Q1157044|lt=Nara Line (JR West)|1=Nara Line (JR West)}} railway. == Overview == The Kofun is situated on a hill on the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|ja|木津川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu River (Kyoto Prefecture)|1=Kizu River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28196671}}, in the northern part of Jōyō City. The surrounding area contains over 100 mounds, including the {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|lt=Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|ja|丸塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|qq=}}. Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27) following the discovery of an intact sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. Several subsequent excavations have been conducted. The mound faces south and features a three-tiered structure. Its overall length is addroximately 180 meters, making it the largest mound in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster and the entire southern Yamashiro region. The mound was encircled by two moats and a double ring of haniwa. == History of Excavations == * 1894 (Meiji 27): Discovery of the sarcophagus and burial goods during railway construction. * 1979 (Showa 54): Designated as a National Historic site. * 1991 (Heisei 3): The excavated sarcophagus was classified as an Important designation1. == burial goods == * '''Sarcophagus burial goods''' ** Mirrors (7 pieces, including {{ill|Triangular-rimmed mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}) ** Stone beads, swords, and other grave goods * '''Haniwa''' ** Cylindrical, house-shaped, and other decorative types == Historic site == === {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} === * Mirrors and sarcophagus found in the Kofun. === Historic site === * Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun (Part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster Historic site) == location == '''location''': Hirakawa Kurumazuka, Jōyō City, Kyoto Prefecture '''Transportation''': - {{ill|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|en|Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q1057349|lt=Kintetsu Kyoto Line|1=Kyoto Line (Kintetsu)}} {{ill|Kutsukawa Station|en|Kutsukawa Station|WD=Q6448547|lt=Kutsukawa Station|1=Kutsukawa Station}} (10 minutes on foot) - {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|en|Nara Line (JR West)|WD=Q1157044|lt=JR Nara Line|1=Nara Line (JR West)}} {{ill|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|en|Shinden Station (Kyoto)|WD=Q974100|lt=Shinden Station (Kyoto)|1=Shinden Station (Kyoto)}} (15 minutes on foot) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|久津川車塚古墳|version=104621243|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:久津川車塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11369464}} [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox ancient site]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Joyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]
